title: anemone alpina subsp. aurea
intro: Anemone alpina subsp. aurea, also known as the golden anemone, is a flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The golden anemone is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Anemone alpina subsp. aurea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
